"""Final debug: understand camera conventions and fix rendering."""
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/opt/triposr')

import os
import json
import torch
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image
import math
import torch.nn.functional as F
from tsr.system import TSR

device = 'cuda:0'

# Load model
model = TSR.from_pretrained(
    "/models/triposr",
    config_name="config.yaml",
    weight_name="model.ckpt",
)
model.renderer.set_chunk_size(131072)
model.to(device)
model.eval()

# Load val object
data_dir = os.environ.get('RAB_DATA_DIR', '/data')
val_dir = os.path.join(data_dir, 'val')
objects = sorted(os.listdir(val_dir))[:3]

for uid in objects:
    obj_dir = os.path.join(val_dir, uid)
    
    with open(os.path.join(obj_dir, 'cameras.json')) as f:
        cameras = json.load(f)
    
    # Print camera params
    print(f"\n=== Object: {uid} ===")
    for c in cameras[:3]:
        print(f"  View {c['view_id']}: az={c['azimuth']:.1f}, el={c['elevation']:.1f}, dist={c['distance']:.3f}")
    
    # Load input
    img = Image.open(os.path.join(obj_dir, 'input.png'))
    if img.mode == 'RGBA':
        # Check alpha
        arr = np.array(img)
        print(f"  Input: shape={arr.shape}, alpha range=[{arr[:,:,3].min()}, {arr[:,:,3].max()}]")
        background = Image.new('RGB', img.size, (255, 255, 255))
        background.paste(img, mask=img.split()[3])
        img_rgb = background
    else:
        img_rgb = img.convert('RGB')
    
    # Check GT
    gt = Image.open(os.path.join(obj_dir, 'target_views', 'view_01.png'))
    gt_arr = np.array(gt)
    print(f"  GT view_01: shape={gt_arr.shape}, alpha range=[{gt_arr[:,:,3].min()}, {gt_arr[:,:,3].max()}]")
    print(f"  GT RGB mean (where alpha>0): {gt_arr[:,:,:3][gt_arr[:,:,3]>128].mean():.1f}")
    print(f"  GT RGB mean (where alpha==0): {gt_arr[:,:,:3][gt_arr[:,:,3]<=128].mean():.1f}" if (gt_arr[:,:,3]<=128).any() else "  No transparent pixels")

# Now test rendering with built-in render method
uid = objects[0]
obj_dir = os.path.join(val_dir, uid)
img = Image.open(os.path.join(obj_dir, 'input.png'))
if img.mode == 'RGBA':
    background = Image.new('RGB', img.size, (255, 255, 255))
    background.paste(img, mask=img.split()[3])
    img_rgb = background
else:
    img_rgb = img.convert('RGB')

with open(os.path.join(obj_dir, 'cameras.json')) as f:
    cameras = json.load(f)

with torch.no_grad():
    scene_codes = model([img_rgb], device=device)

print(f"\nScene codes shape: {scene_codes.shape}")

# Read get_spherical_cameras source
import inspect
from tsr.utils import get_spherical_cameras
print("\n=== get_spherical_cameras source ===")
print(inspect.getsource(get_spherical_cameras))

# Read model.render source
print("\n=== model.render source ===")
print(inspect.getsource(model.render))

# Test: render with built-in method at specific azimuth/elevation
# First, render at view 0's camera to compare with input
cam0 = cameras[0]
print(f"\nView 0 camera: az={cam0['azimuth']}, el={cam0['elevation']}, dist={cam0['distance']}")

# Try rendering at view 0 params using model.render
# model.render takes n_views and renders evenly spaced azimuths
# We need to understand what params it uses

# Let's try rendering 1 view and see what we get
with torch.no_grad():
    frames = model.render(scene_codes, n_views=1, return_type='pt')
    print(f"\nRender output type: {type(frames)}")
    if isinstance(frames, dict):
        for k, v in frames.items():
            if isinstance(v, torch.Tensor):
                print(f"  {k}: shape={v.shape}, range=[{v.min():.3f}, {v.max():.3f}]")
    elif isinstance(frames, list):
        print(f"  List of {len(frames)} items")
        for i, f in enumerate(frames[:3]):
            if isinstance(f, torch.Tensor):
                print(f"  [{i}]: shape={f.shape}, range=[{f.min():.3f}, {f.max():.3f}]")
    elif isinstance(frames, torch.Tensor):
        print(f"  Tensor: shape={frames.shape}, range=[{frames.min():.3f}, {frames.max():.3f}]")

# Also render 12 views to see default behavior
with torch.no_grad():
    frames12 = model.render(scene_codes, n_views=12, return_type='pt')
    print(f"\n12-view render: type={type(frames12)}")
    if isinstance(frames12, torch.Tensor):
        print(f"  shape={frames12.shape}")
    elif isinstance(frames12, list):
        print(f"  List of {len(frames12)} items, first shape={frames12[0].shape if isinstance(frames12[0], torch.Tensor) else type(frames12[0])}")
